nodes.context: Italian American Mafia crime family based in Cleveland, Ohio, involved in labor racketeering, casino skimming, loansharking, and illegal gambling.
Italian American gang formed in Cleveland's Little Italy around 1913, focused on bootlegging and later became the core of the Cleveland crime family.
Italian American gang operating around St. Clair Avenue and E. 152nd Street in Collinwood, Cleveland.
Sicilian immigrant brothers who broke away from the Lonardos and formed their own faction, competing for control of the Cleveland underworld.
Sicilian immigrant brothers who initially led the Cleveland crime family before being killed by the Porrellos.
Network of powerful criminals including Lucky Luciano and Meyer Lansky, formed in 1931.
Mafia's governing body, of which Frank Milano was a charter member.
Chicago-based Mafia family with ties to the Cleveland family under Scalish.
New York Mafia family with ties to the Cleveland family under Scalish.
Pittsburgh-based Mafia family that had an arrangement with the Cleveland family regarding Youngstown rackets.
Midwestern Mafia family that partnered with Cleveland and Milwaukee families in Las Vegas casino skimming.
Midwestern Mafia family that partnered with Cleveland and Kansas City families in Las Vegas casino skimming.
Irish gang led by Danny Greene that competed with the Cleveland family for control of rackets in the 1970s.
One of the Five Families of New York City, with a faction in Rochester, New York.
Italian American Mafia organization based in Detroit, Michigan.
Italian American Mafia family based in New Jersey.
Outlaw motorcycle club, listed as an ally of the Cleveland crime family.
Irish gang in Cleveland, rival of the Cleveland crime family.

edges.context: The Mayfield Road Mob evolved into the Cleveland crime family under Frank Milano in the 1930s.
The Porrellos broke away from the Lonardos in 1926 and killed Joseph and John Lonardo in 1927 to take over the Cleveland family.
The Porrellos were rivals of the Mayfield Road Mob (which became the Cleveland family) and were eliminated by Frank Milano in 1932.
Frank Milano joined the National Crime Syndicate in 1931, aligning the Cleveland family with Luciano and Lansky.
Frank Milano was a charter member of The Commission, the Mafia's governing body.
Under Scalish, the Cleveland family developed ties with the Chicago Outfit.
Under Scalish, the Cleveland family developed ties with the Genovese crime family.
The Cleveland family had an arrangement with the Pittsburgh family, receiving 25% of profits from Youngstown rackets.
The Cleveland, Kansas City, and Milwaukee families partnered in Las Vegas casino skimming and Teamsters pension fund loans.
The Cleveland, Kansas City, and Milwaukee families partnered in Las Vegas casino skimming and Teamsters pension fund loans.
A violent mob war erupted in the late 1970s between the Cleveland family and Danny Greene's gang, resulting in numerous bombings.
Mob war over Youngstown territory from 1978 to 1981, resulting in twelve murders.
Associate Anthony Delmonti worked with the Bonanno family's Rochester faction.
Cleveland family forged ties with Detroit Partnership in the 21st century.
Cleveland family forged ties with DeCavalcante crime family in the 21st century.
Listed as an ally in the infobox.
Listed as a rival in the infobox.
